de: used attributively, often translated into English as a compound word
or: yet, however, now, that said, as it happens
sol: sol in the solfège scale of C, preceded by fa and followed by la)

car: a single-decked long-distance, or privately hired, bus, a coach

certain: certain: a determined but unspecified amount of
du: contraction of de + le, literally “of the”
courage: chin up! keep going! take heart!

lune: any natural satellite of a planet
a month, particularly a lunar month
au: contraction of à + le, literally “to the, for the, at the”

rouge: a supporter of the Republican Party

nous: the plural personal pronoun in the first person:

issue, matter, topic, problem

plural or singular personal pronoun in the second person

to part with somebody, to leave somebody

life, period in which one is alive, between birth and death
